findings.issue: TDD Anchor section is entirely missing from the task file. Task 6 is a code task producing ~120 LoC of concurrent lifecycle code (Mutex, stale-instance guard, GPU->CPU fallback). The template mandates TDD Anchor for code tasks. The Verification Steps explicitly state 'Unit-tests in Phase 1 –Ĺ–Ķ –Ņ–ł—ą–Ķ–ľ for this task' citing user-spec D8, but the template still requires the section present (even if documenting the skip). More importantly: the tech-spec for Task 6 does not carry a TDD Anchor either, so this propagates a gap from tech-spec.
findings.fix: Add `## TDD Anchor` section after `## What to do`. Either (a) list smoke-grep anchors formatted as test entries, or (b) explicitly document the deferral per user-spec D8 with a rationale block (similar to Task 9's approach). Consistent with Task 9 which does carry the section as a justified no-op.

findings.issue: TDD Anchor section is missing. Task 7 creates code (SanctumApplication.kt, MainActivity.kt, Theme.kt) ‚ÄĒ it is a code task per task-creator rules. Template requires the section for code tasks.
findings.fix: Add `## TDD Anchor` section with either concrete anchors or an explicit justified skip (reference user-spec D8 and list the smoke-greps as verification substitutes), matching the approach used in Task 9.

findings.issue: TDD Anchor section is missing. Task 8 creates ModelManagerViewModel + ModelManagerScreen (Kotlin code). Template requires the section for code tasks. Details mention '–Ě–Ķ —ā–Ķ—Ā—ā–ł—Ä—É–Ķ–ľ Compose UI ... TDD Anchor –≤ –∑–į–ī–į—á–Ķ –ĺ—ā—Ā—É—ā—Ā—ā–≤—É–Ķ—ā –Ĺ–į–ľ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–Ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ' but this rationale belongs inside the TDD Anchor section, not in Details.
findings.fix: Add `## TDD Anchor` section that explicitly documents the no-test decision with reference to user-spec D8 and patterns.md Test Infrastructure section, matching Task 9's N/A-with-justification pattern.

findings.issue: TDD Anchor section is missing. Task 10 creates SanctumApp.kt (Compose code) and modifies MainActivity.kt. It is a code task.
findings.fix: Add `## TDD Anchor` section with explicit N/A justification referencing user-spec D8 (Phase 1 has single unit test ‚ÄĒ AllowlistLoaderTest ‚ÄĒ per Task 4).

findings.issue: Tech-spec Verify-smoke for Task 6 lists four commands. Task file's Smoke section correctly carries all four (compileDebugKotlin, lifecycleMutex.withLock >=4, currentInstance === model.instance >=1, KEY_MAIN_ACTIVITY_FQN >=1) plus adds a fifth (UI-imports boundary check). However, the tech-spec smoke check `grep "KEY_MAIN_ACTIVITY_FQN" core-runtime/.../DownloadRepository.kt ‚Č• 1` is carried but its semantic purpose (validating T6 plumbing integrity after wave-2 integration) is preserved only in a comment. This is a minor concern ‚ÄĒ acceptable ‚ÄĒ but verify the grep target path matches after Task 3 completes.
findings.fix: No fix required; finding is informational. Path is correct per Task 3 output structure. Downgrade to minor if flagged at all ‚ÄĒ listed here for traceability.

findings.issue: Context Files entry for LlmChatModelHelper.kt points at `core-runtime/src/main/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/core/inference/LlmChatModelHelper.kt` but Task 3 (per tech-spec) extracts LlmChatModelHelper into :core-runtime ‚ÄĒ the exact package path `core/inference/` is an assumption not verified against Task 3's spec. Task 6 references same file via a different assumption ('object-—Ā—Ā—č–Ľ–ļ–į –Ĺ–į LlmChatModelHelper'). If Task 3 places it under `core/runtime/` or similar, Task 9 will have a dead context link.
findings.fix: Cross-check path with Task 3's Files to modify after Task 3 is finalized. Either adjust the path in Task 9 Context Files or add a note that the exact path is determined by Task 3. Alternatively wildcard the path hint (e.g. 'core-runtime/.../LlmChatModelHelper.kt').

findings.issue: Implementation hints recommends `GlobalScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO)` as fallback for cleanup in onCleared() with a 'TODO for Phase 2 refactoring' comment. GlobalScope is flagged by code-reviewer as anti-pattern (structured-concurrency violation) and the task provides no DI-scope alternative. Decision T9 mandates Mutex lifecycle management ‚ÄĒ a leaking cleanup coroutine undermines correctness guarantees.
findings.fix: Either (a) introduce `@ApplicationScope CoroutineScope` binding as part of Task 6's CoreRuntimeModule provides (pipe into this task's dependencies), or (b) document a concrete decision that cleanup is fire-and-forget via the registry's own SupervisorJob scope (which Task 6 already creates). Replace GlobalScope reference.

findings.issue: Task delegates download action via `registry.startDownload(model)` in one place and `registry.download(model)` in another (Task 6 interface declares `fun download(model: Model): Flow<ModelDownloadStatus>`). Naming inconsistency between Task 6 contract and Task 8 consumer ‚ÄĒ risks compilation failure or late discovery.
findings.fix: Align Task 8 method references with Task 6's canonical interface method `download(model)`. Remove `startDownload` references (What-to-do step 1 uses `registry.startDownload(model)` with parenthesized disclaimer ‚ÄĒ make this decisive and correct).

findings.issue: Task 8 references `ModelEntry.name`, `model.errorMessage`, `model.sizeInBytes`, `receivedBytes`, `totalBytes` as direct fields, but Task 6 defines `ModelEntry(val model: Model, val downloadStatus: ModelDownloadStatus, val initStatus: ModelInitStatus)` ‚ÄĒ the actual fields live under `model.*` and `downloadStatus.*`. The status matrix in Task 8 treats `downloadStatus` as a bare enum with numeric fields but Task 6 keeps it as Gallery's `ModelDownloadStatus` type (which in Gallery is a class carrying receivedBytes/totalBytes/errorMessage).
findings.fix: Replace bare-field references with correct `entry.model.name`, `entry.downloadStatus.receivedBytes`, `entry.downloadStatus.totalBytes`, `entry.downloadStatus.errorMessage`, `entry.model.sizeInBytes`. Update AC text and What-to-do matrix accordingly to match Task 6's ModelEntry shape.

findings.issue: Task 8 references R.string keys `status_not_downloaded`, `status_downloaded`, `action_download`, `action_cancel`, `action_load`, `progress_bytes_format`, `model_size_gb_format`, `unit_gb`, `status_error_format` ‚ÄĒ but Task 7 defines a different key set: `model_status_not_downloaded`, `model_status_downloaded`, `btn_download`, `btn_cancel`, `btn_load`, `progress_bytes_template`. Keys in Task 8 will fail R.string resolution at compile time.
findings.fix: Harmonize key naming between Task 7 (owner) and Task 8 (consumer). Either (a) update Task 8 to use the exact keys Task 7 declares (`model_status_*`, `btn_*`, `progress_bytes_template`), or (b) update Task 7's strings.xml key list to match Task 8's semantic names. Recommended: adopt Task 7's naming (it is the source of truth per tech-spec wave ordering) and fix Task 8.

findings.issue: Task 9 references `R.string.chat_init_failed_title`, `R.string.back`, `R.string.message_interrupted_suffix` ‚ÄĒ Task 7 declares `chat_load_failed_title`, `btn_back`, `chat_message_interrupted_suffix`. Three key mismatches will cause compile failure.
findings.fix: Update Task 9 What-to-do and Implementation hints to use Task 7's canonical keys: `chat_load_failed_title`, `btn_back`, `chat_message_interrupted_suffix`. Also add `chat_load_failed_download_again` usage if the Failed-state Download button from Task 7's key list is intended (tech-spec says no retry button, but key is declared ‚ÄĒ confirm with Task 7 owner).

findings.issue: Task 7 declares 20 string keys but the consumers (Task 8, Task 9) reference several keys not in Task 7's list AND Task 7 declares keys not used by consumers. Examples: Task 7 has `chat_load_failed_download_again` but Task 9 explicitly says no retry button. Task 7 lacks `model_size_gb_format`, `unit_gb`, `chat_init_failed_title`, `status_error_format`, `action_*` variants referenced by Tasks 8/9.
findings.fix: Task 7 should be the single source of truth for Phase 1 strings. After fixing Tasks 8/9 to use Task 7's canonical keys, remove unused keys from Task 7 (`chat_load_failed_download_again` if retry-button is out of scope) and add any missing ones needed by Tasks 8/9 (e.g. `model_size_gb_format`). Re-align all three tasks in a single pass.

findings.issue: Task 9's ChatViewModel calls `LlmChatModelHelper.runInference(...)` directly from the ViewModel, bypassing ModelRegistry. Task 6's registry exposes `getInstance(modelName): LlmModelInstance?` but not a `runInference` wrapper. This means inference-run errors will not flow through `ErrorLog.e("inference-run", ...)` (tech-spec ¬ß3 mandates this component tag). Acceptance Criteria says '–ĺ—ą–ł–Ī–ļ–į —É–∂–Ķ –∑–į–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ä–ĺ–≤–į–Ĺ–į –ł–∑ ModelRegistry' and 'ModelRegistry —É–∂–Ķ –Ņ–ł—ą–Ķ—ā –≤ ErrorLog' ‚ÄĒ but Task 6's interface does not include runInference, so this assumption is incorrect.
findings.fix: Either (a) add `runInference` wrapper to `ModelRegistry` in Task 6 (recommended ‚ÄĒ keeps single-active-model discipline and centralizes ErrorLog writes), updating Task 6 interface + AC + smoke-greps, or (b) accept that ChatViewModel logs `inference-run` failures itself via an injected `ErrorLog` (adds ErrorLog to Task 9's constructor DI). Whichever chosen, update both tasks consistently.

findings.issue: `verify: [smoke]` declared but Task 7 is user-facing bootstrap ‚ÄĒ MainActivity launch, POST_NOTIFICATIONS prompt, first-time app startup deserve a user-verify step even if covered later in Task 10's full scenario. Currently Verification Steps lacks a User subsection.
findings.fix: Either add `verify: [smoke, user]` with a minimal user-check ('Install APK, launch app, see stub screen, POST_NOTIFICATIONS prompt appears on SDK33+'), or document in task body that user-verify is deferred to Task 10's full scenario (current implicit position).

findings.issue: AC mixes testable behavioral claims with grep-structural claims ('grep ‚Č• 4 –≤—Ö–ĺ–∂–ī–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ļ', 'grep ‚Č• 1 –≤—Ö–ĺ–∂–ī–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ķ', '–Ľ–ł—ā–Ķ—Ä–į–Ľ—Ć–Ĺ–ĺ –≤—č—Ä–į–∂–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ķ currentInstance === model.instance'). Grep-structural anchors verify string presence, not behavior ‚ÄĒ they're substitutes for TDD in a no-test Phase. Task-creator guideline flags this as minor: tests should verify behavior, not text.
findings.fix: Keep the grep-structural ACs (they mirror tech-spec smoke-greps and are the only verification bar Phase 1 offers), but add one or two behavioral ACs describing observable outcomes ('second initialize of the same model after cleanup succeeds', 'concurrent initialize+cleanup serialize without SIGSEGV') to strengthen the review target.

findings.issue: TDD Anchor exists but its body is 'N/A –≤ Phase 1 ‚ÄĒ —Ā–ľ. –ļ–ĺ–ľ–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā–į—Ä–ł–Ļ –≤—č—ą–Ķ –ł user-spec D8' plus an HTML comment. This is acceptable as a justified skip but is the only task in the batch that carries TDD Anchor at all ‚ÄĒ inconsistent with Tasks 6/7/8/10 which omit the section entirely.
findings.fix: After fixing the missing TDD Anchor sections in Tasks 6/7/8/10 (critical findings above), align all five tasks on the same pattern: explicit N/A with user-spec D8 reference.

findings.issue: In Task 6, Verification Steps section appears AFTER Context Files, matching the template order. However Task 6's content table '–ö–ĺ–Ĺ—ā—Ä–į–ļ—ā—č –ľ–Ķ—ā–ĺ–ī–ĺ–≤' is embedded inside What to do and duplicates information that also appears under Details. This creates two sources of truth inside one task file.
findings.fix: Keep method contracts in ONE place (preferably Details ‚Üí Files/Implementation hints area) and reference from What to do, to avoid drift when contracts are edited.

findings.issue: Task 7 bundles five distinct concerns: SanctumApplication, MainActivity stub, Theme, AndroidManifest, strings.xml (20 keys covering all of Phase 1). The title itself admits '–≤–Ķ—Ā—Ć Phase 1'. This violates the 1-3 files heuristic. However, tech-spec explicitly intends this bundling (strings.xml owned by Task 7, consumed read-only by Tasks 8/9) to avoid merge conflicts in Wave 3 parallel tasks. The bundle has logical cohesion (app-bootstrap resources) ‚ÄĒ acceptable per logical-cohesion rule.
findings.fix: No action required. Finding is informational ‚ÄĒ tech-spec rationale for bundling is sound (single-owner strings.xml across parallel Wave 3). Consider adding a one-line note in Task 7 Description explicitly naming this decision so reviewers do not flag split-candidate.

findings.issue: Automated subsection contains two bash commands (`./gradlew :app:assembleDebug` and `./gradlew :app:compileDebugKotlin`) but Task 10 is marked `verify: [user]` ‚ÄĒ Automated section is present without a matching frontmatter flag. Minor frontmatter‚ÜĒbody inconsistency.
findings.fix: Either add `smoke` to `verify:` frontmatter array, or move the two gradle commands into a Smoke subsection (which requires `smoke` in verify anyway), or drop them if the full-scenario user-verify implicitly exercises the build.

findings.issue: No Automated subsection declared, but `./gradlew :app:assembleDebug` is listed as AC-8 ('–ü—Ä–ĺ–Ķ–ļ—ā —Ā–ĺ–Ī–ł—Ä–į–Ķ—ā—Ā—Ź'). Template has Automated section commented 'Always present for code tasks'.
findings.fix: Add Automated subsection with the gradle compile/assemble commands matching AC-8 compilation claim.
